use_case_name: Show EV Driver Final Total Cost After Charging

structured.name: Show EV Driver Final Total Cost After Charging
structured.objective: To show an EV Driver the total cost after the transaction is finished.
structured.description:
An EV Driver stops an ongoing transaction by presenting his identification token (for example
RFID). The transaction is stopped and the total cost of the transaction is shown to the EV Driver.
scenario: 1. The EV Driver presents an IdTokenType to stop the transaction.
2. The Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended)

3. The CSMS responds with TransactionEventResponse containing the total cost of the
transaction.
4. The Charging Station shows the total cost to the EV Driver.
structured.prerequisites:
The Charging Station supports Tariff Information
Ongoing transaction and uses central cost calculation by CSMS.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The EV Driver knows the total cost of the transaction.
Failure postcondition:
The EV Driver does NOT know the total cost of the transaction.
structured.remarks:
If the Charging Station was offline when the transaction ended and the
TransactionEventResponse with totalCost is received when the Charging Station comes back
online some time after that, then there is no use in displaying the cost, because the driver has
likely left already. A similar situation applies when TxStopPoint is defined as
ParkingBayOccupancy, in which case the EV must leave the Charging Station to cause the
transaction to end.
The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for stop transaction being configured as follows.
TxStopPoint: ParkingBayOccupancy, EVConnected, Authorized
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might stop at another
moment, which might change the sequence in which message are send. For more details see the
use case: E06 - Stop Transaction options

requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Ended) to the CSMS.

requirements.precondition:
I03.FR.01 AND
When Total Cost is known to the CSMS.
requirements.definition:
The CSMS SHALL send the total cost of the transaction in the
totalCost field of the TransactionEventResponse message.

requirements.precondition:
I03.FR.02 AND
Charging Station was online when transaction
stopped
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL display the total cost to the EV
Driver.

To indicate a free transaction, the CSMS SHALL set totalCost to
0.00. Thus omitting totalCost does not imply that the transaction
was free.

requirements.precondition:
I02.FR.02 AND
TxStopPoint is defined as
ParkingBayOccupancy
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHOULD NOT display the total cost to the
EV Driver. (Driver has left already).

chunks.content:
# I03 - Show EV Driver Final Total Cost After Charging

**Objective:** To show an EV Driver the total cost after the transaction is finished.

**Description:** An EV Driver stops an ongoing transaction by presenting his identification token (for example
RFID). The transaction is stopped and the total cost of the transaction is shown to the EV Driver.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS, EV Driver

**Scenario:**
1. The EV Driver presents an IdTokenType to stop the transaction.
2. The Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended)
3. The CSMS responds with TransactionEventResponse containing the total cost of the
transaction.
4. The Charging Station shows the total cost to the EV Driver.

**Prerequisites:** The Charging Station supports Tariff Information
Ongoing transaction and uses central cost calculation by CSMS.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The EV Driver knows the total cost of the transaction.
Failure postcondition:
The EV Driver does NOT know the total cost of the transaction.

**Remarks:** If the Charging Station was offline when the transaction ended and the
TransactionEventResponse with totalCost is received when the Charging Station comes back
online some time after that, then there is no use in displaying the cost, because the driver has
likely left already. A similar situation applies when TxStopPoint is defined as
ParkingBayOccupancy, in which case the EV must leave the Charging Station to cause the
transaction to end.
The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for stop transaction being configured as follows.
TxStopPoint: ParkingBayOccupancy, EVConnected, Authorized
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might stop at another
moment, which might change the sequence in which message are send. For more details see the
use case: E06 - Stop Transaction options

metadata.heading: I03 - Show EV Driver Final Total Cost After Charging
metadata.use_case_name: Show EV Driver Final Total Cost After Charging

chunks.content:
# I03 - Requirements

**I03.FR.01**
Precondition: When transaction is stopped
The Charging Station SHALL send a TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Ended) to the CSMS.

**I03.FR.02**
Precondition: I03.FR.01 AND
When Total Cost is known to the CSMS.
The CSMS SHALL send the total cost of the transaction in the
totalCost field of the TransactionEventResponse message.

**I03.FR.03**
Precondition: I03.FR.02 AND
Charging Station was online when transaction
stopped
The Charging Station SHALL display the total cost to the EV
Driver.

**I03.FR.04**
To indicate a free transaction, the CSMS SHALL set totalCost to
0.00. Thus omitting totalCost does not imply that the transaction
was free.

**I03.FR.05**
Precondition: I02.FR.02 AND
TxStopPoint is defined as
ParkingBayOccupancy
The Charging Station SHOULD NOT display the total cost to the
EV Driver. (Driver has left already).

metadata.heading: I03 - Requirements
metadata.use_case_name: Show EV Driver Final Total Cost After Charging
